The Ancient Art

Bharatanatyam —
Where Devotion
Becomes Dance

❧

Bharatanatyam is one of the oldest classical dance forms of India, born in the temples of Tamil Nadu over two thousand years ago. It is a language of the body — of nritta (pure rhythm), nritya (expressive gesture), and natya (dramatic narrative) woven into a seamless offering of beauty and devotion.

Every glance, every footfall, every extension of the hand carries meaning — invoking gods, telling stories, and connecting the human spirit to the eternal. To witness an Arangettam is to witness a dancer stepping, for the first time, fully into this ancient river of light.

The Master

The Guru

"The guru is the bridge between tradition and the dancer's own soul — a keeper of the eternal flame."

Nrityalaya School of Dance has nurtured Andrea's journey through years of disciplined practice, patience, and devotion. The guru's guidance transforms a student into a vessel of the art — transmitting not just technique, but the very spirit of Bharatanatyam as it has been passed down through generations of master dancers.

Tonight, we honour this sacred relationship between teacher and student as it culminates in its most beautiful expression: the Arangettam.

The Student

Andrea
Chalissery

✦

You are warmly invited to witness a sacred milestone — a dancer's first emergence into the eternal light of the stage. Andrea has spent years in devoted preparation: countless hours of riyaz, of surrendering to the rhythm, of finding stillness within motion.

The Arangettam is not merely a performance. It is an initiation — a declaration that this dancer is ready to carry the lamp of Bharatanatyam forward. We are honoured to share this moment with you.

Debut · June 27, 2026
